description_long: This helmet is a medieval-style great helm, featuring a rounded dome and a flat, angular faceplate with a pronounced ridge running from the top to the bottom. The front faceplate has a narrow horizontal eye slit for vision and a cross-shaped cutout on one cheek, likely for ventilation or decoration. The sides of the helmet are reinforced with visible rivets and a hinge-like mechanism, possibly for securing the faceplate or for additional structural support. The rear of the helmet is smooth and rounded, providing full coverage for the head. The surface appears to be made of metal, with a matte, slightly rough finish. There are no visible logos, graphics, or modern features, giving it a utilitarian and historical appearance.
description: A medieval-style great helm with a rounded dome, angular faceplate, and cross-shaped cheek cutout, made of matte metal. The helmet is plain, fully enclosing the head with minimal decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view shows the angular faceplate, narrow eye slit, and cross-shaped cutout on the left cheek.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the rounded dome, hinge mechanism, and the cross-shaped cutout.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the smooth, rounded back of the helmet with no openings or decorations.
description_view_3: Right side view reveals the hinge mechanism and the solid, unadorned cheek area.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: One cross-shaped cutout on the left cheek; no other visible vents.
